section_name: Obedience Required to Police Officers, School Crossing Guards, and Escort Flaggers
text:
A person may not wilfully fail or refuse to comply with a lawful order or direction of:

(1) a police officer;

(2) a school crossing guard who:

(A) is performing crossing guard duties in a school crosswalk to stop and yield to a pedestrian; or

(B) has been trained under Section 600.004 (Training of School Crossing Guard) and is directing traffic in a school crossing zone; or

(3) an escort flagger who is directing or controlling the flow of traffic in accordance with a permit issued by the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles under Subtitle E for the movement of an oversize or overweight vehicle.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 542. Gen. Provisions
##### § 542.501 — Obedience Required to Police Officers, School Crossing Guards, and Escort Flaggers

A person may not wilfully fail or refuse to comply with a lawful order or direction of:

    (1) a police officer;

    (2) a school crossing guard who:

        (A) is performing crossing guard duties in a school crosswalk to stop and yield to a pedestrian; or

        (B) has been trained under Section 600.004 (Training of School Crossing Guard) and is directing traffic in a school crossing zone; or

    (3) an escort flagger who is directing or controlling the flow of traffic in accordance with a permit issued by the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles under Subtitle E for the movement of an oversize or overweight vehicle.

*Acts 2019, 86th Leg., R.S., Ch. 1129 (H.B.2620), Sec. 2, eff. September 1, 2019.*

an escort flagger who is directing or controlling the flow of traffic in accordance with a permit issued by the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles under Subtitle E for the movement of an oversize or overweight vehicle.
